title: swank.nvim configuration
description: Configuration schema for swank.nvim — a Neovim-native Common Lisp development environment built on the Swank protocol.

leader.description: Key prefix for all swank.nvim keymaps. Must be a valid Neovim key sequence string.

server.description: Swank server connection settings for manual/external server connections.

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the Swank server.

port.description: Port of the Swank server. Only used when connecting to an external server (autostart = false or swank.attach()).

autostart.description: Controls automatic startup of a local CL implementation + Swank server when a Lisp buffer is opened.

enabled.description: If true, swank.nvim will automatically launch the configured CL implementation and connect on first FileType lisp/cl event.

implementation.description: Name or full path of the CL implementation binary to launch. Basename is used for flag selection: sbcl, ccl, ecl, and abcl have built-in flag profiles. Unknown implementations fall back to SBCL-style flags.

position.description: Where to open the REPL window. 'auto' selects based on available layout space: resolved width ≥80 columns → right, else resolved height ≥12 lines → bottom, else float.

size.description: REPL size as either a fraction of the editor width (for right/left) or height (for top/bottom/float) when in the range (0, 1], or as a fixed width/height in columns/rows when greater than 1.

floating.description: Settings for floating windows (completions, documentation, REPL in float mode).

border.description: Border style for floating windows. Accepts the standard named styles or a custom border array, matching the 'border' option of nvim_open_win() — see :h nvim_open_win().

contribs.description: List of Swank contrib modules to load on connect, in keyword symbol format (e.g. ':swank-repl'). Passed to swank:load-contribs on the server side.
